title: Seat-map legend doesn't mark booked seats with a stable selector
steps_to_reproduce: Search DEL -> BOM, select any flight
Inspect an unavailable (grey) seat in the seat map
Note it has no disabled/aria-disabled attribute or data-testid distinguishing it from an available seat
expected: Unavailable seats are identifiable via a stable attribute (disabled, aria-disabled, or data-testid) so automated tests don't rely on color/position.
actual: Did not reproduce: with the app actually running, unavailable seat buttons carry both a real `disabled` attribute and a stable `data-testid="seat-<id>"`, plus an accessible name ending in "unavailable". This entry was filed from the initial exploration template without verifying against a live instance; TC-FARESEAT-002 has been hardened to assert on `[data-testid^="seat-"][disabled]` directly.

title: Forgot-password error message reveals whether a login ID is registered (account enumeration)
steps_to_reproduce: Submit a registered email with no login ID
expected: Response is identical (or equally generic) regardless of whether the login ID is registered, to prevent enumerating valid accounts
actual: Unregistered login ID shows "No account found for that email, mobile number, or username", while a registered one proceeds to "Reset link generated" - an attacker can use this to check whether an email/mobile/username has an account
